notes: P6-P8 target build-scripts/generate-html.js (NEVER_PUSH infrastructure). Those patches are stranded separately for operator manual application per PROP-016 Mech A.

patches.find: OTS timestamps status_history.json, which bundles prediction parameters together with observation results in a single file. The hash proves when the bundled file existed — not that the prediction preceded the observation. A genuine prospective timestamp would hash an isolated prediction document separately, before any confirming data is added.
patches.replace: OTS timestamps status_history.json, which per-domain bundles prediction-side fields (formula, predicted value, tolerance threshold, source label) together with observation-side fields (observed value, error percentage, pass/fail) at every commit. The structural prediction logic — the code that computes the predicted field — lives in mutable monitor.py source. The OTS hash proves the bundled file existed at the timestamp; it does not prove the prediction-side fields preceded the observation-side fields they are paired with. A genuine prospective timestamp would hash an isolated prediction document separately, before any confirming data is added.
patches.rationale: EXP-290: Harmonize OTS boilerplate with audited contents of status_history.json (fetched 2026-05-06). Old text 'bundles prediction parameters together with observation results' is broadly correct but imprecise. New text names the actual fields (formula, predicted, tolerance_pct, source vs observed, error_pct, pass) and clarifies that both sides land in same commit — so OTS hash covers bundle, not temporal ordering.

patches.find: ss/fail audit results, and statistical comparisons. This is the observation side of the record. The prediction parameters themselves — the formulas, expected values, and tolerances — live in monitor.py source code and docs/model.html, which are only git-versioned, not blockchain-timestamped.
patches.replace: ss/fail audit results, and statistical comparisons — but each per-domain entry also carries its prediction-side fields (formula, predicted value, tolerance threshold, source label) bundled in the same file at every commit. The OTS hash covers the bundle, not a temporal ordering between the prediction-side and observation-side fields. The structural prediction logic — the code that computes the predicted field — lives in monitor.py source code and is mirrored as static text in docs/model.html, both git-versioned, neither blockchain-timestamped.
patches.rationale: EXP-290 P9: Part 1 prose previously stated status_history.json contains only the observation side. Audit confirms it also contains prediction-side fields (formula, predicted, tolerance, source) per domain per commit. Updated to match audited reality.

patches.find: the OTS anchoring timestamps status_history.json, which bundles prediction parameters together with observation results in a single file. The hash therefore covers the entire file state at commit time — not an isolated prediction document.
patches.replace: the OTS anchoring timestamps status_history.json, whose per-domain entries bundle prediction-side fields (formula, predicted value, tolerance threshold, source label) together with observation-side fields (observed value, error percentage, pass/fail) at every commit. The hash therefore covers the entire bundled file state — not an isolated prediction document, and gives no temporal ordering between a prediction text and the observation it is paired with.
patches.rationale: EXP-290 P10: Part 6 OTS infrastructure paragraph updated to match audited file contents.

patches.find: fail audit results, and statistical comparisons. This is the reference data side of the ledger. The prediction parameters — formulas, expected values, and tolerances — live in monitor.py source code and docs/model.html, which are only git-versioned, not blockchain-timestamped.
patches.replace: fail audit results, and statistical comparisons — alongside, in the same per-domain entries, the prediction-side fields (formula, predicted value, tolerance threshold, source label) committed at the same time. The bundled file is what gets blockchain-anchored; the OTS hash covers both sides but provides no temporal ordering between them. The structural prediction logic — the code that computes the predicted field — lives in monitor.py source code and is mirrored as static text in docs/model.html; both are only git-versioned, not blockchain-timestamped.
patches.rationale: EXP-290 P11: Part 8 paragraph updated to match audited file contents. Note: find string is keyed on trailing fragment unique within part8.
